candidate_problem_record.subject: Physics
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: The graph below shows the external force applied to a 15-kg object throughout a displacement of 8.0 m. Calculate the speed of the object after the work was done on it.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集更可能以单解题为主，不强推多解 agent，只保留基础可验证性与可标注性检查。

metadata.explanation_zh:
利用动能公式可求出最终速度:

$K_f = \frac{1}{2}mv_f^2$

将公式重新整理以求解$v_f$:

$v_f = \sqrt{\frac{2K_f}{m}}$

代入已知值$K_f = 192\ \mathrm{J}$和$m = 15\ \mathrm{kg}$:

$v_f = \sqrt{\frac{2(192\ \mathrm{J})}{15\ \mathrm{kg}}} \rightarrow v_f = 5.1\ \mathrm{m/s}$

normalized_value.title: SciVerse 开放题
normalized_value.rewritten_question_text: The graph below shows the external force applied to a 15-kg object throughout a displacement of 8.0 m. Calculate the speed of the object after the work was done on it.

sample_understanding_summary.rationale: The graph is clear and the intended task is understandable: use the force-displacement graph to compute work and then infer speed. However, the prompt appears to omit a needed initial-condition detail (such as initial speed), and the provided answer 5.1 does not match the visible graph data if the object starts from rest. So the sample is understandable only partially, not fully reliable.
